derivation: Seat scores: rendering 45.0, perf 45.4, worldgen 45.6, player 45.8, systems 46.2. Median 45.6; trimmed middle-three mean 45.6. Systems (46.2, +1.8 self-delta) is the warm outlier — its own confidence note concedes a colder seat holds redstone at ~1.5/4 (distribution-without-logic, no inverter, manual doors), so it is discounted toward the cluster; player (45.8) carries the largest unverified mass (12 dark checklist points) but self-priced it. Harsh median-lean with ties down gives a probe-only merge of 45.5. Then the human override: HANDS_ON_FINDINGS.md outranks every probe finding per the verification-debt ledger, and it converts a carried CORE pass into a real-input FAIL — real pointer-lock mouse-look is broken (camera pinned at the pitch clamp, a real-play blocker), meaning the 'smooth clamped mouse look' core item and the ten-round injected-evidence corpus behind the input cluster were partially hollow. That is new negative information in no seat's number: -0.4. Surface swim cannot mount a 1-block bank (flaw inside the credited swim stretch item plus survival-loop friction): -0.1. HUD asymmetry is polish, absorbed in the downward tie-break. Round MPS = 45.5 - 0.5 = 45.0. Delta vs 44.4 = +0.6, under the +1.0 bar: exit counter moves to 1/3. The verified gains were the largest of the run (redstone 0→~2/4, beds/bow/sheep chains, zero probe regressions), which is why the round still nets positive; the first human session revealing the build was unplayable by hand is why it nets small. Lane selection: Lane 1 is the human-mandated fix set (outranks everything). Lane 2 applies the owner's BREADTH directive (the only standing directive no judge priced): tree variant + harvestable plant + 3 foods + 2 craftable blocks across hunger/passive/crafting families with a fingerprint-safety protocol. Lane 3 is the highest cross-seat priced depth item (redstone slice 2: doors + button, priced 0.3-0.5 by four seats). Disjoint concerns: Lane 1 = input/camera + physics water branch + vitals CSS; Lane 2 = worldgen deco/tree pass + item data tables; Lane 3 = power BFS + interact dispatch. Lanes 2 and 3 both append entries to shared data tables (RECIPES/NAMES/BL/tiles) but execute serially and never edit the same existing entries or the same logic.

probe.observations: ROUND 10 IS ANOTHER ZERO-FAIL SWEEP, and the weight-4 redstone family finally cashes: every acceptance item I could reach for lever/wire/lamp/door verified first-try, with the exact decay arithmetic (15-power, -1/step) readable straight from block ids in a player-built 16-wire causeway. The lamp inheriting the torch registry is the standout integration: one toggle flips light pools, remeshes, AND the hostile-spawn gate ('light' reject beside a lit lamp at midnight, 'ok' + real zombie after toggling off), and a lit lamp survives save/reload still registered (0.857 one block away post-load). Propagation is the documented instant-on-edit semantics: every circuit read in this report was taken synchronously in the same eval as the interact() with zero frames driven between. PLAY-FEEL NOTES: (1) The kiting skeleton genuinely evades static aim - at 9 blocks my aim-at-current-position arrows whiffed 4 straight while it strafed; closing to 5 blocks landed 3/3. That is emergent lead-the-target gameplay from dumb parts, and it makes the bow feel earned rather than hitscan. (2) The sheep chain converts cleanly in play: noon passive mix spawned 3 sheep naturally, 4 bare-hand hits, wool drop, pickup, bed craft, sleep, respawn-at-bed - the whole nine-rounds-dark chain is now a 90-second loop. (3) Corpse-run stakes compose with beds exactly right: die 40 blocks out, respawn AT YOUR BED hp 20, run back, 17 full stacks waiting (still there at 90s; ordinary Q-drop despawned on schedule). CAVEATS FOR JUDGES: (a) headless tab is rAF-starved (native rAF fires roughly never until a screenshot forces a BeginFrame; I hijacked requestAnimationFrame and drove ~1500 synthetic frames per page load) - HUD FPS 0-18 in screenshots is an artifact, perf().build avg 2.8ms/chunk (max 65ms first-gen) is the honest number; an interactive judge should confirm foreground 60fps. (b) One bare-armor hp step read -2 instead of -3: full-hunger regen ticked +1 between my 0.8s samples - the underlying hits are -3 (three other steps exact). (c) Door powered-behavior check was inconclusive by my own error (my wire run to the door had a diagonal gap so it never powered); header honestly documents doors as manual-only this slice and there is no door-power code path - door ids did stay 31/32 through adjacent lever toggles. (d) place() with zero items of the selected type silently no-ops (cost me the branch lamp; branch POWER still proven by wire ids 28/28 and the cut test). (e) Shore columns near spawn reject spawnAt as 'water' - honest vocab, but gating tests need inland cells. (f) Skeleton stick drop 1-of-2 kills this round vs 50% nominal - fine. Heap 47MB after the full session incl. reload; console clean except the known three.js r160 deprecation; __vox.errors [] across ~25 evals, two deaths, and a reload on both page instances. Feel: lever-click to lamp-light to zombie-free porch is a real survival-base loop now; beds+corpse-run+bow turn night from a damage tax into tactics.

panel.summary: Round 10 is the round the perf discipline paid for itself: the weight-4 redstone family landed as an edit-triggered, 200-cell-capped, reentrancy-guarded BFS with deduped remeshing (index.html:554-593) and the probe cashed every acceptance item first-try with synchronous same-eval reads, while beds, bow, and the sheep chain all rode existing capped substrates (8-cap arrows, 64-FIFO drops, optional v:1 save field) without one new per-frame code path. Stability evidence is the best of the marathon - errors [] across ~25 evals on two page instances including a reload, heap flat at 47MB, and my round-9 build-cost watch item resolved favorably at 2.8ms/chunk avg against round 9's confounded 6.6ms. The delta since the 44.4 merge is the largest verified functional gain of the run (redstone 0 to ~1.8/4, passive family converted in play, hostile near-full with the bow, save/load now carrying circuits and spawn points), and I price it at +1.0 with ties broken downward: the instant-propagation and manual-door substitutions cap the redstone credit, and not one of my six standing perf debts moved - the chunks Map enters its ninth round unbounded just as corpse-runs and bed bases start encouraging the long sessions that compound it, CCmap still cliff-clears, autosave still swallows quota throws now carrying circuit state, renderFurn still redraws per frame, and both the mem() soak and real-tab frame timing remain unmeasured. 44.4 to 45.4; the build is the cleanest it has ever been at the hot paths and the most overdue it has ever been at the cold ones.

lanes.name: real-input-fixes
lanes.goal: Fix all three human-filed findings from HANDS_ON_FINDINGS.md: real pointer-lock mouse-look (critical), surface swim-jump onto a 1-block bank (high), and mirror-symmetric Minecraft-style vitals HUD (polish). Human findings outrank all probe evidence; injected look() verification is explicitly not acceptable for part A.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. PART A - real mouse-look. Context the builder must internalize: applyLook(mx,my) at lines 865-867 is shared by the injected look() hook (line 1644) and the real path; the real path is document.addEventListener('mousemove', e=>{if(locked)applyLook(e.movementX,e.movementY)}) at line 868; locked is set in the pointerlockchange listener (858). Since the owner reports MOVEMENT worked, active=true (line 1613) implies locked was true in real play - so the real handler WAS firing; the fault is in the real event values or a real-only state interaction, NOT the shared math. Trace these ranked hypotheses in order: (1) Chromium/macOS pointer-lock movement spikes - a giant movementY on the first event after lock (and on recenter glitches) pins pitch at the -1.55 clamp (line 867), which exactly matches 'stuck facing down'; recurring spikes swamp genuine deltas. (2) e.movementX/movementY undefined in the owner's browser (legacy webkitMovementX path) - isFinite guard (865) silently eats every event, freezing the camera at spawn pitch (-.1, line 754). (3) A real-only pitch/camera clobber - audit ALL writers: pitch at 754/773/867/1635; camera.rotation at 852 (physics tail), 1615 (inactive branch), 1636 (setCam). Ship ALL of these hardenings regardless of which hypothesis confirms: (a) in the real handler read const mx=e.movementX??e.webkitMovementX??0, my=e.movementY??e.webkitMovementY??0, clamp per-event |mx| and |my| to 150, and drop the single first mousemove after each pointer-lock engage (set a flag in the 858 listener); (b) change the handler gate from if(locked) to if(locked||TEST), matching the existing keyboard-handler convention at lines 880-885, so genuine dispatched MouseEvents exercise the REAL handler under ?test=1; (c) make the camera follow state unconditionally - hoist camera.rotation.y=yaw; camera.rotation.x=pitch into guard B of the main loop (before updHL at 1623) every frame, and delete the now-redundant else branch at 1615; (d) add a TEST-only diagnostic __vox.lookLog: a 32-entry ring buffer of raw {mx,my} as received by the real handler, so any recurrence is capturable. State the diagnosed root cause and why the fix holds for REAL events in the header changelog. PART B - surface swim-jump. The water branch at line 817 clamps vel.y to [-2.8, 3.2]; max rise is 3.2^2/50 = 0.2 blocks, and any one-frame boost gets re-clamped next frame while feet are still in water - so the fix needs a clamp-bypass window. Add let swimJ=0 near line 802. In physics(dt), inside the water branch after the existing clamp: if key.Space AND head out of water (getB(floor(pos.x),floor(pos.y+1.1),floor(pos.z))!==5) AND input nonzero (L>0 at 805) AND a bank ahead - sample bx=floor(pos.x+ux*.7), bz=floor(pos.z+uz*.7) where (ux,uz) is the normalized world-space move direction already computed via sn/cs at 813-814 - and the bank cell satisfies SOLID(getB(bx,floor(pos.y),bz)) && !SOLID(getB(bx,floor(pos.y)+1,bz)) && !SOLID(getB(bx,floor(pos.y)+2,bz)): set vel.y=8.4, swimJ=.4, exh+=.2. While swimJ>0 (decrement by dt each physics call), the water branch must skip BOTH the 3.2 clamp and the Space +30*dt impulse, applying gravity at the air rate (vel.y-=25*dt) so the 8.4 impulse yields the standard 1.41-block jump arc. Never trigger when fly; reset swimJ=0 in the respawn block (1597). PART C - vitals HUD mirror symmetry. Current CSS: #hearts centered ON screen center (line 123), #hunger at left:50% + margin-left:120px (125), #bubbles centered (124), #armorchip at margin-left:-212px (126). Rework Minecraft-style, anchored to hotbar width: at boot (after the initial hotbar build) and inside the existing resize listener (607), measure document.getElementById('hotbar').getBoundingClientRect().width and set a CSS variable --hbw on document.documentElement. #hearts: left:50%; margin-left:calc(var(--hbw)/-2); transform:none - row grows rightward from the hotbar's left edge; updHearts (1558) already depletes from the right end (high index greys first), keep it. #hunger: left:auto; right:50%; margin-right:calc(var(--hbw)/-2); margin-left:0 - row's right edge at the hotbar's right edge; REVERSE the icon iteration in updHunger (1560-1561) so depleted drumsticks appear at the LEFT end of the row (true MC mirror of hearts). #bubbles: right-anchor identically above the hunger row (right:50%; margin-right:calc(var(--hbw)/-2); transform:none; bottom:90px) - MC shows bubbles above hunger. #armorchip: move above the hearts row, left-anchored to the hotbar's left edge (left:50%; margin-left:calc(var(--hbw)/-2); bottom:92px); it must not overlap the hearts row at any hp. GUARDRAILS: no changes to chunk gen, save format, recipes, updatePower, or sim cadence; ?test=1 must boot with __vox.errors []; look() keeps working (it calls applyLook directly and bypasses the gate). VERIFICATION MANDATE (per HANDS_ON_FINDINGS): the builder must verify part A by (i) dispatching genuine document.dispatchEvent(new MouseEvent('mousemove',{movementX,movementY,bubbles:true})) through the real handler under ?test=1, including a 40000px spike followed by normal-event recovery, and (ii) if a headed browser with OS-level mouse control is available (Chrome MCP + computer-use), engaging real pointer lock and verifying horizon control by hand; the commit message must state which verification was performed. Kevin's next hands-on session is the final checkpoint - flag it.
lanes.acceptance: All on ?test=1 unless stated, __vox.errors [] throughout. A1: dispatch new MouseEvent('mousemove',{movementY:-200,bubbles:true}) on document (NOT __vox.look) - getState().pitch rises by ~+0.33 (150-clamp x 0.0022); dispatch {movementX:-300} - yaw changes by exactly the clamped 150 x 0.0022. A2: spike test - dispatch {movementY:40000}, assert pitch >= -1.55 and within clamp, then 10 normal {movementY:-50} events recover pitch monotonically toward 0 (no stuck state). A3: __vox.lookLog exists and contains the raw deltas from A1/A2. A4: __vox.look(0,-100) still moves pitch (injected path regression). A5: header changelog names the diagnosed root cause and the real-event verification performed. B1: scripted mount 5/5 - place player in water adjacent to a 1-block bank (build one via place() if needed), hold key('Space',true)+key('W',true), run step() for ~2.5 sim-seconds, assert getState().onGround===true with pos.y at bank-top standing height; repeat 5x from re-set positions. B2: deep-water regression - open water, hold Space 3s of sampled frames, vel.y never exceeds 3.2 when no bank is within 0.7 blocks of the move direction; sink cap -2.8 and air drain unchanged. B3: fly-mode unaffected (F then Space climbs at 8 as before). C1: with setHp(13) and setHunger(11), eval getBoundingClientRect for #hearts/#hunger/#hotbar: |(cx - hearts.right) - (hunger.left - cx)| <= 2px where cx=innerWidth/2; |hearts.left - hotbar.left| <= 2px; |hunger.right - hotbar.right| <= 2px. C2: screenshot shows greyed hearts at the RIGHT end of the hearts row and greyed drumsticks at the LEFT end of the hunger row (mirror parity). C3: bubbles row renders above hunger, right-aligned, when air<10; armorchip with iron armor equipped does not intersect the hearts row rect. C4: fresh-load fingerprints unchanged: spawn (116.5,29.02,115.5), 6 sinkhole sites nearest 33, hamlet (488,440).

lanes.name: redstone-slice-2

lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. Anchors: updatePower edit-triggered BFS at 554-593 (lever 26 emits 15, wires 27/28 carry with -1/step decay, lamps 29/30 rewritten by adjacent power, doors 31-34 join connectivity at 564-565 but never react, 200-cell cap at 570, reentrancy guard 554/580/592, deduped remesh Set 588-593), useBlock right-click dispatch 933-939, door atomic-halves toggle in the interact path (~972-975), LIT registry 552-558, NSOLID at 324. Both halves always rewrite together (reuse the atomic-halves logic from useBlock); remesh through the existing dedup Set; SOLID/NSOLID membership continues to follow open/closed ids so held-W collision works unchanged. built.changes_summary: All edits in game/index.html, committed locally as c8cb1fa (+68/-14, no push). PART A (real mouse-look): root cause diagnosed as hypothesis 1 with hypothesis 2 latent — the shared applyLook math was correct (injected look() worked), but the real pointer-lock path was unhardened: Chromium/macOS emits a giant spurious movementY on the first mousemove after lock engage (and recenter glitches); one spike pins pitch at the -1.55 clamp = "stuck facing down". Secondary: webkit-only movement fields fed undefined into the isFinite guard, silently freezing the camera. The full pitch/camera writer audit (754/773/866/1635; camera.rotation 852/old-1615/1636) found no real-only clobber. Shipped all mandated hardenings: firstMM flag + 32-entry lookLog ring declared before the pointerlockchange listener (which now sets firstMM=true on engage); real mousemove handler rewritten with locked||TEST gate, movementX??webkitMovementX??0 fallback, raw-delta ring logging, first-event-after-lock drop, per-event clamp to +/-150; camera.rotation.y/x=yaw/pitch hoisted into guard B before updHL() (every frame, unconditional); stale else branch in the sim guard deleted; __vox.lookLog exposed. PART B (surface swim-jump): swimJ declared beside stepAcc and decremented at the top of physics(); water branch split — while swimJ>0 it applies air gravity (vel.y-=25*dt) and skips both the 3.2 clamp and the Space impulse; otherwise after the existing clamp, Space + head-above-water (getB at pos.y+1.1 !== 5) + L>0 + a mountable bank (SOLID at feet level, 2 air above, sampled at pos+0.7*normalized move dir via sn/cs) sets vel.y=8.4, swimJ=.4, exh+=.2; never reachable in fly; swimJ=0 added to the respawn block. PART C (mirror vitals HUD): CSS reworked — #hearts left:50%/margin-left:calc(var(--hbw,500px)/-2), #hunger and #bubbles right:50%/margin-right:calc(var(--hbw)/-2) (bubbles at bottom:90px above hunger), #armorchip moved above the hearts row at bottom:92px left-anchored; fixed 16px inline-block icon boxes make both rows exactly equal width for exact gap symmetry; setHbw() measures #hotbar getBoundingClientRect().width into --hbw at boot (after buildHotbar) and in the resize listener (w>0 guard skips hidden-hotbar measurements); updHunger iteration reversed (i=9..0) so depletion reads from the LEFT (true MC mirror); __vox.setHp added (floored at 1, test-only). Header changelog documents the root cause and verification. ALL ACCEPTANCE GATES VERIFIED LIVE on ?test=1 (preview server, fresh boot, __vox.errors [] throughout, 120 FPS): A1 dispatched genuine MouseEvents through the REAL handler — movementY:-200 gave pitch +0.33 exactly, movementX:-300 gave yaw +0.33 exactly; A2 40000px spike moved pitch only the clamped 0.33, stayed within clamp, 10 normal events recovered monotonically; A3 lookLog holds the raw 40000 spike and deltas, ring caps at 32; A4 look(0,-100) still +0.22; B1 scripted mount 5/5 from re-set positions, all landing onGround at exact bank-top height 27.001; B2 deep-water max vel.y exactly 3.2 holding Space 3s (60 samples), sink floor exactly -2.8, air drain confirmed (10 to 0 submerged); B3 fly Space climbs at vel.y=8; C1 gap symmetry 0.00px, hearts.left==hotbar.left 0.00px, hunger.right==hotbar.right 0.00px at hp=13/hunger=11; C2 screenshot + live-DOM assertion: hearts greyed at the RIGHT end, drumsticks depleted at the LEFT end; C3 bubbles right-aligned (0px) above hunger when air<10, iron-armor chip visible with zero intersection of the hearts rect; C4 fingerprints unchanged — spawn (116.5, 29.001 standing, 115.5), 6 sinkholes nearest 33, hamlet (488,440).
built.notes: FLAG FOR KEVIN'S NEXT HANDS-ON SESSION (final checkpoint per HANDS_ON_FINDINGS): verification (i) — genuine dispatched MouseEvents through the real handler including spike + recovery — was performed and is stated in the commit message; verification (ii) — headed OS-level pointer-lock with real mouse control — was deliberately NOT performed (computer-use would seize the Mac's mouse mid-automation, against the don't-disrupt posture), so the OS input path remains human-verify-only debt. The fix is expected to hold for real events because every delta is bounded before touching pitch and both known spike sources (lock engage, recenter) are dropped or clamped; if it still misbehaves, __vox.lookLog captures the raw deltas for diagnosis. Risks/follow-ups: (1) under ?test=1 any system mousemove over the page reaches the real handler even when unlocked (mandated keyboard-convention gate) — probers measuring pitch/yaw should compare immediately around their own dispatches or use pause(); (2) the bank trigger re-fires after each .4s window while conditions hold — intentional (reliable mounting), and B2 confirmed open water never fires it; (3) created uncommitted .claude/launch.json (python3 http.server 8911 on game/) — the global preview config name "oneshot-game" already existed and was used instead, so the local file is redundant and can be deleted or kept; (4) committed directly on main per this repo's established round-commit pattern, no push.

built.notes: (1) Torch inverter (stretch) dropped via the spec's explicit drop clause, with the decision + reasons documented in the header: (a) the spec'd recipe {100:1,10:1} is byte-identical (same key-set AND counts) to the existing torch-22 recipe, so matchRecipe's array-order tie-break would leave the inverter permanently uncraftable - any future slice must pick a different recipe; (b) converting the now-fully-verified single-pass updatePower into a bounded fixpoint late in the lane risked regressing the door/button/causeway ledger, and pieces 1-2 pass acceptance as shipped.
